visa_types.name: Visiting Family / Relatives
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/zaf/en/cze/travel-insurance). 
- A filled in checklist. Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/44OPuEqrKiIQm2B2SUhYXk/fbcade804a6b1408abfdbd586768d7f1/visa-checklist-250616.pdf) to download checklist.
- An original application form for Schengen visa (A4) (www.mzv.cz/visaform (http://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/visa_form/index.html)) filled in completely and legibly and signed by the applicant (or the legal guardian for children).
- A proof of payment of the visa fee.
- A passport valid for 90 days after the applicant’s last day of stay in Schengen territory, with two
blank visa pages for the visa sticker. Please note that the Czech Republic DOES NOT accept South African Temporary passports.
- A copy of the passport (only the data page with the photograph and the signature).
- A recent passport sized photograph (3,5 x 4,5 cm) in colour to be glued, do not staple.
- A proof of financial funds for the stay at the territory of Schengen states, e.g. recent bank account statements showing movements over the last 3 months.
- A round trip air-ticket and itinerary with dates and flight numbers specifying an entry to and an exit from the territory of Schengen states. If more than one entry is required, a reason must be provided.
- Proof of a valid travel health insurance for the entire duration of the stay in the Schengen territory – a letter from the insurance company covering medical expenses, including emergency hospital treatment and repatriation (transportation) to the home country/country of residence, stating the name of the person, the policy number, the period of cover and the medical coverage equivalent of 30.000 EUR.
- Proof of accommodation in all Schengen countries visited during the trip.
- Visa of the final / main (with the longest stay) country of destination must be obtained (if needed) before applying for a Schengen visa.
- A certified parental consent from both parents is required for children under the age of 18 travelling alone. If a child travels with only one parent, the other parent must produce a notarised (certified) consent. For pupils and students – an original letter from a school, a college or a university.
- Official Invitation from the person to be visited, if applicable, certified by the Alien Police inspectorate in the Czech Republic (it is required when a physical or a legal person invites the applicant to the Czech Republic and provides accommodation, financial support or other from the requirements list).
- Spouses of EU/EEA citizens and their children under the age of 21: Full Marriage or Full Birth certificates
must be submitted with the application as a proof of the relationship with a EU/EEA spouse/parent.
visa_types.visa_fees:
The consular fees for Embassy services payable in Rands.

[cze > zaf > en > Table:Tourism > Visa Fees 1]

*Please use your Passport Number as Reference.
Persons exempt from visa fee: 
- Members of the immediate family of a Czech Republic citizen (spouse and children),
- Members of families of citizens of EU Member States and EEA,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in official capacity,
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when traveling to school, university and professional training,
- Researchers travelling to do research as noted in Recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and Council of 28 September 2005,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25 travelling to attend seminars, conferences, sport, culture or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
- For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa fee is 35 EUR.
Please Note:
- The applicable fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change
- All fees are non-refundable.
- The Visa Application Centre offers the following services at a nominal additional cost:
- Courier services
- SMS Tracking Facility
- Premium Lounge Service
- Photo booth service
- All service charges are to be paid at the Visa Application Centre through Debit/Credit card only in South African ZAR (VFS service charge and other charges)
visa_types.processing_time:
VISA ISSUANCE PROCEDURE AND TIMELINES
A visa application cannot be submitted earlier than 6 months prior to the beginning of the planned journey and can be submitted by:
- Applicant himself
- For minors by a parent or legal guardian
Standard visa processing time for South Africa, Angola, Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Madagascar, Mauritius, Mozambique, and Namibia passport holders is 15 calendar days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For Nigeria, Bangladesh and Ghana applicants, the processing time for both passport holders will be 45 days from the day application is received at the Embassy.
Standard visa processing time for passport holders other than the above listed countries is minimum 30 days (and up to 60 days)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visit to family or friend visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for visiting your family member or friend. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visit to family or friend visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for visiting your family member or friend. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days

## Visa Fees
The consular fees for Embassy services payable in Rands.

[cze > zaf > en > Table:Tourism > Visa Fees 1]

*Please use your Passport Number as Reference.
Persons exempt from visa fee: 
- Members of the immediate family of a Czech Republic citizen (spouse and children),
- Members of families of citizens of EU Member States and EEA,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in official capacity,
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when traveling to school, university and professional training,
- Researchers travelling to do research as noted in Recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and Council of 28 September 2005,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25 travelling to attend seminars, conferences, sport, culture or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
- For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa fee is 35 EUR.
Please Note:
- The applicable fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change
- All fees are non-refundable.
- The Visa Application Centre offers the following services at a nominal additional cost:
- Courier services
- SMS Tracking Facility
- Premium Lounge Service
- Photo booth service
- All service charges are to be paid at the Visa Application Centre through Debit/Credit card only in South African ZAR (VFS service charge and other charges)

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/zaf/en/cze/travel-insurance). 
- A filled in checklist. Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/44OPuEqrKiIQm2B2SUhYXk/fbcade804a6b1408abfdbd586768d7f1/visa-checklist-250616.pdf) to download checklist.
- An original application form for Schengen visa (A4) (www.mzv.cz/visaform (http://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/visa_form/index.html)) filled in completely and legibly and signed by the applicant (or the legal guardian for children).
- A proof of payment of the visa fee.
- A passport valid for 90 days after the applicant’s last day of stay in Schengen territory, with two
blank visa pages for the visa sticker. Please note that the Czech Republic DOES NOT accept South African Temporary passports.
- A copy of the passport (only the data page with the photograph and the signature).
- A recent passport sized photograph (3,5 x 4,5 cm) in colour to be glued, do not staple.
- A proof of financial funds for the stay at the territory of Schengen states, e.g. recent bank account statements showing movements over the last 3 months.
- A round trip air-ticket and itinerary with dates and flight numbers specifying an entry to and an exit from the territory of Schengen states. If more than one entry is required, a reason must be provided.
- Proof of a valid travel health insurance for the entire duration of the stay in the Schengen territory – a letter from the insurance company covering medical expenses, including emergency hospital treatment and repatriation (transportation) to the home country/country of residence, stating the name of the person, the policy number, the period of cover and the medical coverage equivalent of 30.000 EUR.
- Proof of accommodation in all Schengen countries visited during the trip.
- Visa of the final / main (with the longest stay) country of destination must be obtained (if needed) before applying for a Schengen visa.
- A certified parental consent from both parents is required for children under the age of 18 travelling alone. If a child travels with only one parent, the other parent must produce a notarised (certified) consent. For pupils and students – an original letter from a school, a college or a university.
- Official Invitation from the person to be visited, if applicable, certified by the Alien Police inspectorate in the Czech Republic (it is required when a physical or a legal person invites the applicant to the Czech Republic and provides accommodation, financial support or other from the requirements list).
- Spouses of EU/EEA citizens and their children under the age of 21: Full Marriage or Full Birth certificates
must be submitted with the application as a proof of the relationship with a EU/EEA spouse/parent.

## Photo Specifications
Photographs must follow the below guidelines
- in colour
- 35x45 mm
- printed on normal photographic paper
- taken against a light background
- full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to the head of hair
- the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed
- The photograph of a person, who for religious or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the presented photos do not follow these requirements your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
VISA ISSUANCE PROCEDURE AND TIMELINES
A visa application cannot be submitted earlier than 6 months prior to the beginning of the planned journey and can be submitted by:
- Applicant himself
- For minors by a parent or legal guardian
Standard visa processing time for South Africa, Angola, Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Madagascar, Mauritius, Mozambique, and Namibia passport holders is 15 calendar days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For Nigeria, Bangladesh and Ghana applicants, the processing time for both passport holders will be 45 days from the day application is received at the Embassy.
Standard visa processing time for passport holders other than the above listed countries is minimum 30 days (and up to 60 days)

## Download Form
Applicants, who wish to obtain a Czech visa application form, can download the requisite form here.
Please note the instructions mentioned below while taking the printout. 
Download Visa Applications Forms

New Visa Application Form Visa C (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5xbfIKqgYLBAtYQgkZdtdB/9f4b22afdcc50d1c4c96a3ad5899555e/Application_for_schengen_visa.pdf)
New Visa Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3wP7kXvMt5TBxM8BH7Dhgk/26db434377c8921d75fa4bdb60864519/checklist-for-schengen-visa-application.pdf)
Checklist for Schengen visa application EU citizen family member (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/nElA7Hbb4lDv0ZuSbyCa3/4ea7b1ae4e4a039044f605f1ea8b0092/checklist-for-schengen-visa-application-eu.pdf)
Checklist for Schengen visa application EU citizen family member (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/nElA7Hbb4lDv0ZuSbyCa3/4ea7b1ae4e4a039044f605f1ea8b0092/checklist-for-schengen-visa-application-eu.pdf)
Checklist for Schengen visa application Airport (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5VZPcZYNTWpcZzfbLycGFQ/76c875169da6e5ec815ac93c09a8985a/checklist-for-schengen-visa-application-airport.pdf)
Invitation form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2z0L0uIOrIyt5jo2Kr51bg/22ee4c12c7a6911ba8eaef542b76fdda/cze-invitation-form.pdf)

SIS EU Notice (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1SaIgRIjdMWIkPRgbZNmJq/84a5ef55851bbd7e85c8aa01fdb624f9/sis-eu-notice.pdf)
Please Note:
- Number of pages: 3
- You can also obtain this form from the visa application centre.
It is mandatory to be filled by all applicants along with the application form. 

## Download Instructions:
The forms are in PDF format.
You will need an Adobe Acrobat Reader to view and download these forms. Please 

click here (http://get.adobe.com/reader/) to download Adobe Acrobat Reader.

## To download the Application Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.visa_fees:
The consular fees for Embassy services payable in Rands.

[Table:Others]

*Please use your Passport Number as Reference.
Persons exempt from visa fee: 
- Members of the immediate family of a Czech Republic citizen (spouse and children),
- Members of families of citizens of EU Member States and EEA,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in official capacity,
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when traveling to school, university and professional training,
- Researchers travelling to do research as noted in Recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and Council of 28 September 2005,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25 travelling to attend seminars, conferences, sport, culture or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
- For citizens of Albania, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bosnia, Cape Verde, Georgia, Herzegovina, Macedonia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Montenegro, Serbia, Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa fee is 35 EUR.( These Agreements between the European Union and other States essentially determine the application fee at 35 EUR, shorten the visa application processing period, regulate exemptions from visa fees and simplify terms and conditions for issue of visas to specific categories of people. The agreements don´t regulate the terms and conditions of stay over 90 days, which remain to be regulated by national legislation.)
- The complete list of the Facilitation Agreements with the full text of the Agreements:
 
http://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/short_stay_visa/agreements_on_the_facilitation_of_the/index.html (http://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/short_stay_visa/agreements_on_the_facilitation_of_the/index.html)
Please Note:
- The applicable fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change
- All fees are non-refundable.
- The Visa Application Centre offers the following services at a nominal additional cost:
- Courier services
- SMS Tracking Facility
- Premium Lounge Service
- Photo booth service
- All service charges are to be paid at the Visa Application Centre through Debit/Credit card only in South African ZAR (VFS service charge and other charges)

In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For Nigeria, Bangladesh and Ghana applicants, the processing time for both passport holders will be 45 days from the day application is received at the Embassy.
Standard visa processing time for passport holders other than the above listed countries is minimum 30 days (and up to 60 days)

- A proof of financial funds for the stay at the territory of Schengen states, e.g. recent bank account statements showing movements over the last 3 months.
- A round trip air-ticket and itinerary with dates and flight numbers specifying an entry to and an exit from the territory of Schengen states. If more than one entry is required, a reason must be provided.
- Proof of a valid travel health insurance for the entire duration of the stay in the Schengen territory – a letter from the insurance company covering medical expenses, including emergency hospital treatment and repatriation (transportation) to the home country/country of residence, stating the name of the person, the policy number, the period of cover and the medical coverage equivalent of 30.000 EUR.
- Proof of accommodation in all Schengen countries visited during the trip.
- Visa of the final / main (with the longest stay) country of destination must be obtained (if needed) before applying for a Schengen visa.
- A certified parental consent from both parents is required for children under the age of 18 travelling alone. If a child travels with only one parent, the other parent must produce a notarised (certified) consent. For pupils and students – an original letter from a school, a college or a university.
- Official Invitation from the person to be visited, if applicable, certified by the Alien Police inspectorate in the Czech Republic (it is required when a physical or a legal person invites the applicant to the Czech Republic and provides accommodation, financial support or other from the requirements list).
- Spouses of EU/EEA citizens and their children under the age of 21: Full Marriage or Full Birth certificates
must be submitted with the application as a proof of the relationship with a EU/EEA spouse/parent.
visa_types.visa_fees:
The consular fees for Embassy services payable in Rands.

[cze > zaf > en > Table:Tourism > Visa Fees 1]

*Please use your Passport Number as Reference.
Persons exempt from visa fee: 
- Members of the immediate family of a Czech Republic citizen (spouse and children),
- Members of families of citizens of EU Member States and EEA,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in official capacity,
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when traveling to school, university and professional training,
- Researchers travelling to do research as noted in Recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and Council of 28 September 2005,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25 travelling to attend seminars, conferences, sport, culture or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
- For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa fee is 35 EUR.
Please Note:
- The applicable fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change
- All fees are non-refundable.
- The Visa Application Centre offers the following services at a nominal additional cost:
- Courier services
- SMS Tracking Facility
- Premium Lounge Service
- Photo booth service
- All service charges are to be paid at the Visa Application Centre through Debit/Credit card only in South African ZAR (VFS service charge and other charges)
visa_types.processing_time:
VISA ISSUANCE PROCEDURE AND TIMELINES
A visa application cannot be submitted earlier than 6 months prior to the beginning of the planned journey and can be submitted by:
- Applicant himself
- For minors by a parent or legal guardian
Standard visa processing time for South Africa, Angola, Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Madagascar, Mauritius, Mozambique, and Namibia passport holders is 15 calendar days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For Nigeria, Bangladesh and Ghana applicants, the processing time for both passport holders will be 45 days from the day application is received at the Embassy.
Standard visa processing time for passport holders other than the above listed countries is minimum 30 days (and up to 60 days)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Tourist visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for tourism purposes. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Tourist visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for tourism purposes. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days

## Visa Fees
The consular fees for Embassy services payable in Rands.

[cze > zaf > en > Table:Tourism > Visa Fees 1]

*Please use your Passport Number as Reference.
Persons exempt from visa fee: 
- Members of the immediate family of a Czech Republic citizen (spouse and children),
- Members of families of citizens of EU Member States and EEA,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in official capacity,
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when traveling to school, university and professional training,
- Researchers travelling to do research as noted in Recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and Council of 28 September 2005,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25 travelling to attend seminars, conferences, sport, culture or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
- For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa fee is 35 EUR.
Please Note:
- The applicable fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change
- All fees are non-refundable.
- The Visa Application Centre offers the following services at a nominal additional cost:
- Courier services
- SMS Tracking Facility
- Premium Lounge Service
- Photo booth service
- All service charges are to be paid at the Visa Application Centre through Debit/Credit card only in South African ZAR (VFS service charge and other charges)

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/zaf/en/cze/travel-insurance). 
- A filled in checklist. Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/44OPuEqrKiIQm2B2SUhYXk/fbcade804a6b1408abfdbd586768d7f1/visa-checklist-250616.pdf) to download checklist.
- An original application form for Schengen visa (A4) (www.mzv.cz/visaform (http://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/visa_form/index.html)) filled in completely and legibly and signed by the applicant (or the legal guardian for children).
- A proof of payment of the visa fee.
- A passport valid for 90 days after the applicant’s last day of stay in Schengen territory, with two
blank visa pages for the visa sticker. Please note that the Czech Republic DOES NOT accept South African Temporary passports.
- A copy of the passport (only the data page with the photograph and the signature).
- A recent passport sized photograph (3,5 x 4,5 cm) in colour to be glued, do not staple.
- A proof of financial funds for the stay at the territory of Schengen states, e.g. recent bank account statements showing movements over the last 3 months.
- A round trip air-ticket and itinerary with dates and flight numbers specifying an entry to and an exit from the territory of Schengen states. If more than one entry is required, a reason must be provided.
- Proof of a valid travel health insurance for the entire duration of the stay in the Schengen territory – a letter from the insurance company covering medical expenses, including emergency hospital treatment and repatriation (transportation) to the home country/country of residence, stating the name of the person, the policy number, the period of cover and the medical coverage equivalent of 30.000 EUR.
- Proof of accommodation in all Schengen countries visited during the trip.
- Visa of the final / main (with the longest stay) country of destination must be obtained (if needed) before applying for a Schengen visa.
- A certified parental consent from both parents is required for children under the age of 18 travelling alone. If a child travels with only one parent, the other parent must produce a notarised (certified) consent. For pupils and students – an original letter from a school, a college or a university.
- Official Invitation from the person to be visited, if applicable, certified by the Alien Police inspectorate in the Czech Republic (it is required when a physical or a legal person invites the applicant to the Czech Republic and provides accommodation, financial support or other from the requirements list).
- Spouses of EU/EEA citizens and their children under the age of 21: Full Marriage or Full Birth certificates
must be submitted with the application as a proof of the relationship with a EU/EEA spouse/parent.

## Photo Specifications
Photographs must follow the below guidelines
- in colour
- 35x45 mm
- printed on normal photographic paper
- taken against a light background
- full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to the head of hair
- the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed
- The photograph of a person, who for religious or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the presented photos do not follow these requirements your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
VISA ISSUANCE PROCEDURE AND TIMELINES
A visa application cannot be submitted earlier than 6 months prior to the beginning of the planned journey and can be submitted by:
- Applicant himself
- For minors by a parent or legal guardian
Standard visa processing time for South Africa, Angola, Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Madagascar, Mauritius, Mozambique, and Namibia passport holders is 15 calendar days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For Nigeria, Bangladesh and Ghana applicants, the processing time for both passport holders will be 45 days from the day application is received at the Embassy.
Standard visa processing time for passport holders other than the above listed countries is minimum 30 days (and up to 60 days)

## Download Form
Applicants, who wish to obtain a Czech visa application form, can download the requisite form here.
Please note the instructions mentioned below while taking the printout. 
Download Visa Applications Forms

New Visa Application Form Visa C (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5xbfIKqgYLBAtYQgkZdtdB/9f4b22afdcc50d1c4c96a3ad5899555e/Application_for_schengen_visa.pdf)
New Visa Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3wP7kXvMt5TBxM8BH7Dhgk/26db434377c8921d75fa4bdb60864519/checklist-for-schengen-visa-application.pdf)
Checklist for Schengen visa application EU citizen family member (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/nElA7Hbb4lDv0ZuSbyCa3/4ea7b1ae4e4a039044f605f1ea8b0092/checklist-for-schengen-visa-application-eu.pdf)
Checklist for Schengen visa application EU citizen family member (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/nElA7Hbb4lDv0ZuSbyCa3/4ea7b1ae4e4a039044f605f1ea8b0092/checklist-for-schengen-visa-application-eu.pdf)
Checklist for Schengen visa application Airport (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5VZPcZYNTWpcZzfbLycGFQ/76c875169da6e5ec815ac93c09a8985a/checklist-for-schengen-visa-application-airport.pdf)
Invitation form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2z0L0uIOrIyt5jo2Kr51bg/22ee4c12c7a6911ba8eaef542b76fdda/cze-invitation-form.pdf)

SIS EU Notice (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1SaIgRIjdMWIkPRgbZNmJq/84a5ef55851bbd7e85c8aa01fdb624f9/sis-eu-notice.pdf)
Please Note:
- Number of pages: 3
- You can also obtain this form from the visa application centre.
It is mandatory to be filled by all applicants along with the application form. 

## Download Instructions:
The forms are in PDF format.
You will need an Adobe Acrobat Reader to view and download these forms. Please 

click here (http://get.adobe.com/reader/) to download Adobe Acrobat Reader.

## To download the Application Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Business visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for business purposes. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days. This visa will be issued for business
                                                                        purpose only; for gainful professional activities (even if the work is not financially rewarded) you have to apply for a work permit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Business visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for business purposes. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days. This visa will be issued for business
                                                                        purpose only; for gainful professional activities (even if the work is not financially rewarded) you have to apply for a work permit.
